name: Syngonium podophyllum 'Albo Variegatum'
origin: Cultivated variegated selection of Syngonium podophyllum, a species native to Mexico through tropical America.
aboutText: Syngonium podophyllum 'Albo Variegatum' is a highly sought-after variegated cultivar derived from the widely distributed species native to the rainforests of Mexico and tropical America. This vining hemiepiphyte is prized for its striking aesthetic, characterized by high-contrast white sectoral variegation against deep green foliage. As it matures, the plant exhibits distinctive arrowhead-shaped leaves with variable lobing, climbing actively via aerial roots. Thriving in warm, humid environments, this selection brings a dramatic architectural element to indoor collections.

quickFacts.matureSize: Can reach several meters as a climber

morphology.leafShape: Arrowhead-shaped; entire when juvenile, lobed when mature
morphology.variegation: Albo, with clean white sectors and green marbling
